column1: # confusables_supplement.tsv — cross-script confusable mappings NOT in TR39.
# TR39 confusables.txt leaves some visually-identical cross-script pairs with no
# shared prototype, so disarm's normalize_confusables() returns the source
# unchanged and a spoofed identifier never collides with its target. This file
# supplements TR39 with measured cross-script equivalences, folded through
# scripts/gen_confusables.py with PRIORITY over the generated entries (for both
# the latin and cyrillic targets). It covers the additive Greek/Cyrillic pairs
# (#342) and the bare-iota vertical-bar re-point (#343), both carved from #336.
# Provenance: the in-scope pairs and the danger weights are taken from the
# measured confusable-vision dataset © Paul Wood FRSA (@paultendo)
# (confusable-weights-v2.json, 4,174 pairs, v2026-03-02), CC-BY-4.0, applying the
# selection threshold danger >= 0.97 (the OQ3 decision on #336).
# Columns (tab-separated):
#   source   hex codepoint of the non-target source character
#   latin    latin-target fold ("-" = no latin override; keep the generated value)
#   cyrillic cyrillic-target fold ("-" = no cyrillic override)
#   danger   confusable-vision danger weight for the pair (informative)
#   note     the pair / decision this row encodes
# All rows are ADDITIVE (fill a pass-through gap) except the #343 ι row (03B9,
# both columns), which RE-POINTs the bare Greek iota from the i-class to the
# l/vertical-bar class.
# source
03AF
column5: ί GREEK SMALL LETTER IOTA WITH TONOS / і (pair 1)
в CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER VE → b (pair 2 latin; cyrillic is self)
п CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER PE → n (pair 5 latin; cyrillic is self)
π GREEK SMALL LETTER PI → n (transitive closure {η,п,π})
χ GREEK SMALL LETTER CHI / х (pair 8)
ω GREEK SMALL LETTER OMEGA → w (pair 9 latin; cyrillic ꙍ already)

column1: 03CA
column5: ϊ GREEK SMALL LETTER IOTA WITH DIALYTIKA / ї (pair 10)
ї CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER YI → i (pair 10 latin; cyrillic is self)

column1: 03CC
column5: ό GREEK SMALL LETTER OMICRON WITH TONOS / о (pair 11)
ѻ CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER ROUND OMEGA / о (pair 13)
β GREEK SMALL LETTER BETA → cyrillic в (pair 2 cyrillic; latin b via #341 ß→b)
η GREEK SMALL LETTER ETA → cyrillic п (pair 5 cyrillic; latin n already)
ѡ CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER OMEGA → ꙍ (pair 9 cyrillic; latin w already)
ι GREEK SMALL LETTER IOTA → l / ӏ (#343 re-point; was i / і)
